hero.title: More Than Childcare:
hero.subtitle: The club for everyone raising children.
hero.description: Trusted care for families, real careers for caregivers, and reliable staffing and training for the programs that serve them—all inside one club.

joinCards.parentSub: Find care and learn with TRC.

howItWorks.title: How it Works
howItWorks.step1Desc: Use our AI-powered matching to find the perfect fit for your family's needs.
howItWorks.step2Desc: We conduct thorough background checks and verification for everyone.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Connect with vetted professionals and build lasting relationships of trust.
howItWorks.heading: How It Works
howItWorks.intro: Getting started with The Raising Club is simple. In three steps, families, caregivers, and programs connect inside one club for trusted care, real careers, and better-staffed programs.

features.sectionDesc: Our platform brings together everything needed to build safe, stable, high-quality care around each child.
features.feature1Body: We verify IDs and references, offer optional background checks, and surface practical safety guidance—so families can feel confident and at peace.
features.feature2Body: We match families, caregivers, and programs—including families with families and caregivers with caregivers—so each child's circle of care fits their needs.
features.feature3Body: Create or join nanny shares, learning pods, and after-school care with families who share your neighborhood, schedule, and priorities.
features.feature4Body: Educators bring degrees & certifications; TRC adds science-backed training and badges that make all expertise visible to families and programs at a glance.
features.feature5Body: Daycares, preschools, and after-school programs use TRC to hire new educators and upskill existing teams, keeping classrooms and activities reliably staffed.
features.feature6Body: Access TRC guidance when questions come up, and manage payments in one place with clear rates and fewer awkward money talks.

audiences.parentTagline: Find caregivers and other families who feel like part of your parenting team—so you're supported in raising your child, not juggling it all alone.
audiences.caregiverTagline: Raise children with purpose, and build a real career in early childhood while you do it.
audiences.organizationTagline: Solve staffing and training for your daycare, preschool, and after-school programs in one place.
audiences.sectionSubtitle: One club where the adults around each child connect, collaborate, and grow together.

parentPoints.heading: Care You Can Feel Confident In
parentPoints.body: See caregivers vetted for safety and experience, then use smart filters to find someone who fits your child and your family—not just your schedule.

parentPoints.heading: Learn & Grow Together with TRC
parentPoints.body: Learn about child development with TRC, and invite the nanny you already love into TRC training so you share the same approach.

parentPoints.heading: The Kind of Help Your Family Actually Needs
parentPoints.body: Choose caregivers whose strengths match your priorities—whether that’s early learning support, tutoring, home organization, or family meals.

parentPoints.heading: Build Your Small Village
parentPoints.body: Connect with nearby families for nanny shares, shared care, and micro-programs, so your child has a stable circle of care and friendship.

caregiverPoints.heading: Stand Out as a Professional
caregiverPoints.body: Create a profile that highlights your experience, training, and specialties so families and programs know you’re the right hire.

caregiverPoints.heading: Grow with TRC Training & Badges
caregiverPoints.body: Earn TRC badges in child development and specialized family support to unlock premium, higher-paying roles.

caregiverPoints.heading: Fair Pay, Clear Expectations
caregiverPoints.body: Find roles where pay, hours, and responsibilities are transparent from the start.

caregiverPoints.heading: Families, Programs & Community That Fit You
caregiverPoints.body: Connect with families, daycares, centers, and fellow TRC caregivers—for roles, playdates, and a professional community that respects your work and shares your values.

organizationPoints.heading: Trained & Credentialed Educators from Day One
organizationPoints.body: Hire early childhood and out-of-school educators and caregivers with verified degrees, certifications, or TRC training.

organizationPoints.heading: Build a Reliable Staffing Bench with TRC Filters
organizationPoints.body: Use TRC filters to keep a ready pool of floaters, substitutes, and out-of-school staff matched to your age groups and hours, so you don’t have to cancel classes or close rooms at the last minute.

organizationPoints.heading: Upskill the Team You Already Have
organizationPoints.body: Enroll your classroom, after-school, and enrichment staff in TRC training and badges to raise quality in every setting.

organizationPoints.heading: A Program Parents Brag About
organizationPoints.body: Show families a stable, trained team grounded in child development and out-of-school learning that sets you apart.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: We believe that quality childcare should be accessible, affordable, and empowering for everyone. The Raising Club is building the infrastructure to make that possible.
mission.weAreLabel: At The Raising Club, we are:

pillars.body: Children need more than supervision; they need emotionally steady, well-prepared adults who build deep self-trust and competence—so they can grow into their fullest selves.
Families deserve an upgrade to their whole ecosystem—where quality care and real understanding of children are the norm, not the exception—so raising children feels shared, not lonely.
Nannies, professional caregivers, and educators deserve respect, training, and real careers—so they can build stable, dignified lives.
Care for children must be treated as essential infrastructure—so work and opportunity aren’t limited by who can afford care, and the next generation grows up ready to lead.

cta.title: Ready to Join?
cta.description: Start your journey with The Raising Club today.

hero.description: The Raising Club brings families, caregivers, and programs together with evidence-based guidance—so raising children feels less overwhelming and grounded in what helps them thrive.

founder.name: Ara V.
founder.p1: Araceli “Ara” Vazquez is an entrepreneur and educator transforming how childcare and early education are understood, delivered, and valued. Raised in Mexico in a family shaped by resilience and entrepreneurship—one that saw education as a path to opportunity—she brings both personal conviction and strategic vision to her work.
founder.p2: As the founder of The Raising Club, she is building modern infrastructure for childhood, connecting families, caregivers, and organizations around a higher standard of care. With a background in Business Administration, a certification in Montessori education, and more than eight years of experience building a successful bilingual education program for children in New York City, Ara brings together an entrepreneurial mind and deep expertise in child development.
founder.p3: Through her work, she is raising both the standard and the economics of childcare—helping caregivers earn more, families access better options, and organizations operate with stronger, more efficient models. She is helping redefine childcare as a high-value sector with the power to drive meaningful economic and social progress at scale.

items.body: Every recommendation is grounded in child development science.
A real village: parents, caregivers, grandparents, and educators.
Designed for minorities, women, and disabled communities.

cta.headingAccent: and nobody should do it alone.
cta.description: Join a growing community of intentional families, trained caregivers, and programs building something better—together.

manifesto.label: Our Manifesto
manifesto.heroDescription: We're not building just an app. We're building modern infrastructure for childhood—a global club where every adult around a child belongs.
manifesto.columnOneP1Rest: shouldn't feel improvised and lonely. But families are being asked to do everything—work, logistics, the mental load—while childcare still runs on patches.
manifesto.columnOneP2Rest: is building modern infrastructure for childhood: a membership platform where education, connection, and community live in one experience. Every adult around a child has a place here—parents, caregivers, grandparents, educators, and extended family.
manifesto.columnOneP3Rest: We take the best of child development science—brain development, learning, nutrition, language, and mental health—and turn it into guidance rooted in skills, regulation, and repair. That becomes daily practice: consistent routines, clear boundaries without yelling, environments that prevent chaos, and communication tools that reduce frustration.
manifesto.columnTwoP1Rest: We also rebuild the "village" for real life. TRC connects families with screened, trained caregivers and supports care setups that actually work: one-on-one care, nanny shares, pods, playdates, and local networks—made easier with clear agreements, defined roles, and a path that adapts as life changes.
manifesto.columnTwoP2Rest: When you raise the standard of care, you change more than childhood. You professionalize caregiving careers, reduce parental anxiety, and sustain workforce participation—especially for mothers—because raising a child stops being a private problem and becomes shared infrastructure.
manifesto.quote: Change childhood, and the future gets built differently.

marketplacePage.heroDescription: A single marketplace connecting caregivers seeking opportunities, parents finding trusted care, organisations building their teams — and everyone finding community.
marketplacePage.connectionsSectionDesc: The Raising Club isn't just a job board. It's a living network where every role finds the right match — including peer-to-peer.
marketplacePage.connectionDesc1: Browse verified nannies, educators, babysitters and specialists by location, availability and age group.
marketplacePage.connectionDesc2: Full-time, part-time, temporary and recurring gigs posted by real families in your area.
marketplacePage.connectionDesc3: Childcare centres and schools access a vetted pool of professionals, with credential verification built in.
marketplacePage.connectionDesc4: Arrange playdates, share recommendations and build a local parent community in your neighbourhood.
marketplacePage.connectionDesc5: Share knowledge, cover shifts, refer clients and grow professionally as part of a peer community.
marketplacePage.whoSectionTitle: What the marketplace does for you
marketplacePage.whoSectionDesc: Every user type gets a tailored experience — browse caregivers, post jobs, or source staff — all within one community.
marketplacePage.roleParentDesc: Find verified, local caregivers and build community with other families around you.
marketplacePage.roleParentFeature1: Browse & filter verified caregivers by distance, rate, age group
marketplacePage.roleParentFeature2: Post your care needs and get matched with caregivers
marketplacePage.roleParentFeature3: Connect with nearby parents — organise playdates & share tips
marketplacePage.roleParentFeature4: Enrol in parenting courses and attend community events
marketplacePage.roleCaregiverDesc: Find work that fits your schedule, grow your skills and build a professional peer network.
marketplacePage.roleCaregiverFeature1: Browse job listings from families — full-time, part-time or gigs
marketplacePage.roleCaregiverFeature2: Get discovered by families and organisations seeking your skills
marketplacePage.roleCaregiverFeature3: Connect with other caregivers — share shifts, referrals and insights
marketplacePage.roleCaregiverFeature4: Take professional development courses and earn verifiable badges
marketplacePage.roleOrganizationDesc: Source qualified caregivers, post open roles, and build a reliable professional pipeline.
marketplacePage.roleOrganizationFeature1: Access a vetted pool of credentialled care professionals
marketplacePage.roleOrganizationFeature2: Post open positions and manage applications in one place
marketplacePage.roleOrganizationFeature3: View caregiver course completions and verified certifications
marketplacePage.roleOrganizationFeature4: Host events and courses to attract and develop talent
marketplacePage.learningTitle: Courses & events connect the whole community
marketplacePage.learningDesc: Learning isn't separate from the marketplace — it's woven in. Courses build credentials caregivers can showcase; events bring all three user types together in real life.
marketplacePage.coursesDesc: Structured courses available to caregivers, parents and organisations. Completed courses appear directly on caregiver profiles as verifiable badges.
marketplacePage.eventsDesc: Local events create real-world touchpoints for all roles. Networking nights, hiring fairs, playdate meetups and hands-on workshops.
marketplacePage.joinCtaTitle: Ready to join the Raising Club?
marketplacePage.joinCtaDesc: Choose your role and create your profile in minutes. Free to get started.
marketplacePage.joinCtaParentPerks2: Post care needs and get matched

marketplacePage.joinCtaOrganizationPerks4: Host events & recruit at community fairs

termsPage.header_intro: Welcome to The Raising Club. Please read these Terms carefully before using our platform. By creating an account or using any part of our services, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use The Raising Club.
termsPage.s1_p1: The Raising Club (TRC, we, us, or our) operates a digital platform at theraisingclub.com that connects families, parents, caregivers, and childcare organizations. Our services include a caregiver marketplace, family-to-family community, job postings, e-courses, events, messaging, and membership plans (collectively, the Platform or Services).
termsPage.s1_p2: The Raising Club is organized into two entities: The Raising Club Marketplace (theraisingclub.com) and The Raising Club Foundation (theraisingclub.org). These Terms govern your use of the Marketplace platform.
termsPage.s2_p1: By registering for an account, clicking Create Account, or otherwise accessing the Platform, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to these Terms and our Privacy Policy. These Terms form a legally binding agreement between you and The Raising Club.
termsPage.s2_p2: If you are accepting these Terms on behalf of an organization, school, or other legal entity, you represent that you have authority to bind that entity to these Terms.
termsPage.s3_li1: You must be at least 18 years old to register as a parent, caregiver, or organization.
termsPage.s3_li2: The Platform is not directed at, and may not be used by, anyone under 13 years of age. Childrens information may only be entered by a parent or legal guardian on their behalf.
termsPage.s3_li3: You must provide accurate, current, and complete information during registration and keep it updated.
termsPage.s3_li4: You may not create an account if you have been previously banned from the Platform or if applicable law prohibits it.
termsPage.s3_li5: By using the Platform, you represent that you meet all eligibility requirements.
termsPage.s4_intro: The Platform has four primary account roles:
termsPage.s4_role_parent: Parent — Families seeking childcare, connection with other families, and access to events and courses.
termsPage.s4_role_caregiver: Caregiver — Individuals offering childcare, babysitting, nanny, or related services, whether seeking paid work or community connection.
termsPage.s4_role_org: Organization — Schools, childcare centers, programs, or other entities offering services or hiring caregivers.
termsPage.s4_role_admin: Admin — Platform administrators only; provisioned internally by TRC.
termsPage.s4_p1: Each role has access to different features and is subject to role-specific obligations described throughout these Terms. You may hold only one role per account. Role selection is made during onboarding and may not be changed after completion without contacting us.
termsPage.s5_p1: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials and for all activity that occurs under your account. You agree to notify us immediately at hello@theraisingclub.com if you suspect unauthorized access to your account. We are not liable for any loss resulting from unauthorized use of your account.
termsPage.s5_p2: Anonymous onboarding: We use a temporary anonymous session for the first steps of onboarding to save your progress before you create a permanent account. Once you provide an email and password, your session is upgraded and your progress is preserved. Anonymous sessions that are never converted are automatically purged after 14 days.
termsPage.s6_title: 6. The Platform — What We Are and Are Not
termsPage.s6_warning: Important: The Raising Club is a technology platform and online community — not a childcare agency, staffing firm, employer, or background-check service. We do not employ caregivers, place caregivers with families, or supervise any childcare arrangements made through the Platform.
termsPage.s6_p1: Families are solely responsible for evaluating, interviewing, selecting, and supervising any caregiver they connect with through TRC. Caregivers are independent individuals, not employees or agents of The Raising Club. All hiring decisions, compensation arrangements, and employment compliance obligations rest with the hiring party.
termsPage.s6_p2: TRC makes no representation or warranty about the suitability, reliability, safety, or qualifications of any caregiver, family, or organization listed on the Platform. Use of the Platform and any resulting engagements are at your own risk.
termsPage.s7_p1: Caregivers may create a public-facing profile that includes their experience, skills, age groups served, certifications, availability, and care settings. Profile information is self-reported by the caregiver and is not independently verified by TRC unless explicitly stated.
termsPage.s7_p2: The TRC Verified and TRC Certified Pro badges, where displayed, indicate only that TRC has processed a third-party verification or credentialing step as described at the time the badge was awarded. These badges do not constitute an endorsement of, or guarantee of the fitness, character, or suitability of, any individual caregiver. Families should independently verify all credentials, references, and qualifications before engaging any caregiver.
termsPage.s7_p3: Caregivers agree not to misrepresent their experience, qualifications, certifications, or availability. TRC reserves the right to remove badges, suspend, or permanently remove profiles that contain inaccurate information.
termsPage.s8_p1: TRC may offer or facilitate access to third-party background check services. Where a background check badge is displayed, it reflects a check conducted at a specific point in time by that third party and is subject to the limitations of that service. Background checks are not a guarantee of safety and may not capture all criminal history, civil records, or other relevant information. Check coverage and depth vary by jurisdiction.
termsPage.s8_p2: Families and organizations are strongly encouraged to conduct their own reference checks, interviews, and trial periods before engaging any caregiver for ongoing care. TRC accepts no responsibility or liability arising from interactions, incidents, or arrangements between users, whether or not a background check was run.
termsPage.s9_p1: Parents and organizations may post care job listings (the Jobs feature) describing their childcare needs, schedule, compensation range, and other requirements. Job posts are visible to authenticated caregivers on the Platform.
termsPage.s9_p2: Caregivers may browse open listings and submit applications. Applications may include a cover note and proposed rate. Submitting an application does not create any guarantee of employment or engagement.
termsPage.s9_p3: Co-hire invitations allow a job owner to invite a caregiver to consider a specific listing collaboratively. The caregiver retains full discretion to accept or decline. Accepting a co-hire invitation does not create a binding employment contract — that is between the parties directly.
termsPage.s9_p4: TRC is not a party to any hiring arrangement, employment agreement, or compensation negotiation between users. All payment, scheduling, and employment terms are set and fulfilled by the parties themselves, outside the Platform.
termsPage.s10_p1: The Connect Families feature allows parents to create an optional, opt-in family listing to connect with other families for playdates, co-hire arrangements, or community support. Family listings are published voluntarily and are visible to other authenticated platform users.
termsPage.s10_p2: Family listings include household information you choose to share (care needs, neighborhood, childrens age ranges, etc.). You control your listings visibility via the publish toggle. You are responsible for the accuracy of your listing and for the safety of any in-person meetups you arrange through the Platform.
termsPage.s10_p3: TRC strongly recommends meeting in public places for first-time connections and using your judgment when arranging in-person gatherings with other Platform users.
termsPage.s11_p1: TRC and participating organizations may offer events through the Platform. Events may be free or paid. For paid events, fees are charged at the time of registration via our payment processor (Stripe).
termsPage.s11_li1: Capacity: Events may have limited capacity, tracked per child registered. Registration is confirmed upon payment (for paid events).
termsPage.s11_li2: Cancellations by attendee: Refund eligibility for event cancellations is determined by the events stated cancellation policy. Unless otherwise stated, event registrations are non-refundable within 48 hours of the event start time.
termsPage.s11_li3: Cancellations by organizer: If an event is cancelled by TRC or the hosting organization, registered attendees will receive a full refund or credit.
termsPage.s11_li4: Changes: Event details (date, time, location, format) may be subject to change. We will notify registered attendees of material changes.
termsPage.s12_p1: TRC offers e-courses that may be free or paid. Upon purchasing a course, you receive a non-transferable, non-exclusive license to access the course content for your personal use.
termsPage.s12_li1: Refunds: Paid course purchases may be refunded within 48 hours of purchase, provided you have not completed more than 20% of the course content. After 48 hours, no refunds are issued. To request a refund, contact hello@theraisingclub.com.
termsPage.s12_li2: Access: Course access is granted for the period described at the time of purchase. TRC reserves the right to retire course content with reasonable notice.
termsPage.s12_li3: Certificates: Completion certificates, where offered, are valid for the period stated. TRC does not guarantee that certificates satisfy any regulatory, licensing, or employment requirement.
termsPage.s13_p1: TRC offers tiered membership plans (including free and paid tiers) that unlock Platform features. Plan pricing, features, and availability are managed by TRC and may be updated over time. Current plan details are available at /membership.
termsPage.s13_li1: Billing: Paid plans are billed through Stripe. By subscribing, you authorize TRC to charge your payment method on a recurring basis at the stated interval (monthly or annual).
termsPage.s13_li2: Renewal: Subscriptions renew automatically unless cancelled before the renewal date. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period; you retain access until then.
termsPage.s13_li3: Price changes: We will provide at least 30 days notice before increasing the price of a plan you are currently subscribed to.
termsPage.s13_li4: Refunds: Subscription fees are generally non-refundable except as required by applicable law. If you believe a charge was made in error, contact us within 30 days.
termsPage.s13_li5: Comp grants: TRC may grant complimentary plan access to certain users at its discretion. Comp grants do not carry any cash value and may be revoked.
termsPage.s13_li6: Failed payments: If a payment fails, we may downgrade your account to the free tier and notify you to update your payment method.
termsPage.s14_p1: Some subscription plans support household membership, which allows a primary account holder to invite additional adults (co-parents, guardians, etc.) to share a family subscription. Invited adults join via a separate sign-up link and occupy a seat counted against the plans household limit.
termsPage.s14_p2: The primary account holder is responsible for the subscription and billing, and accepts these Terms on behalf of the household. Each invited adult must independently agree to these Terms upon sign-up. Removing a household member terminates their access but does not trigger a pro-rated refund.
termsPage.s15_p1: The Platform allows families and caregivers to leave reviews of each other following a connection or engagement. Reviews must be honest, based on first-hand experience, and comply with our community standards.
termsPage.s15_li1: You may not post fake, incentivized, or retaliatory reviews.
termsPage.s15_li2: Reviews must not include personal contact information, defamatory statements, or content that violates any law.
termsPage.s15_li3: TRC may remove reviews that violate these rules but is not obligated to do so and does not verify the accuracy of reviews.
termsPage.s15_li4: TRC makes no representation that reviews reflect the complete experience of using any caregiver or family.
termsPage.s16_p1: The Platform includes a private messaging feature for direct communication between matched users. Messages are stored on our servers to power the service. By using the chat feature, you agree:
termsPage.s16_li1: Not to use messaging to solicit or receive payments outside the Platform in violation of any obligation you have to TRC.
termsPage.s16_li2: Not to send unsolicited messages, spam, or any content that harasses, threatens, or demeans another user.
termsPage.s16_li3: Not to share or solicit personal financial information, passwords, or government ID numbers via chat.
termsPage.s16_li4: To treat all communications with other users with respect, consistent with our community standards.
termsPage.s16_p2: TRC does not actively monitor private messages but reserves the right to review messages in response to a reported violation or as required by law.
termsPage.s17_p1: User Content means any profile information, photos, listings, job posts, review text, course content (for organization accounts), chat messages, or other material you submit to the Platform.
termsPage.s17_p2: You retain ownership of your User Content. By submitting it, you grant TRC a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use, host, store, display, and distribute that content solely to operate and improve the Platform. This license ends when you delete the content or your account, subject to any legal hold or regulatory obligation.
termsPage.s17_p3: You represent and warrant that you own or have the necessary rights to the content you submit, that it does not infringe any third partys intellectual property, and that it complies with these Terms and applicable law.
termsPage.s18_li1: Post false, misleading, or fraudulent information on your profile, listings, or reviews.
termsPage.s18_li2: Impersonate any person or entity or misrepresent your affiliation with any person or entity.
termsPage.s18_li3: Harass, threaten, discriminate against, or harm any other user on or off the Platform.
termsPage.s18_li4: Solicit or engage in transactions intended to circumvent TRCs payment or subscription systems.
termsPage.s18_li5: Scrape, crawl, or systematically collect data from the Platform without written permission.
termsPage.s18_li6: Upload or distribute viruses, malware, or other harmful code.
termsPage.s18_li7: Use the Platform for any unlawful purpose or in violation of any applicable regulation.
termsPage.s18_li8: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the Platform or another users account.
termsPage.s18_li9: Use the Platform to market competing services or recruit users to a competing platform.
termsPage.s18_li10: Post, share, or solicit any content involving the exploitation of minors in any form.
termsPage.s18_li11: Use automated tools, bots, or scripts to interact with the Platform.
termsPage.s18_p1: Violations may result in immediate suspension or permanent termination of your account.
termsPage.s19_p1: The safety of children and families is our highest priority. If you encounter behavior that you believe endangers a child, constitutes abuse, or violates applicable law, you should contact local authorities immediately. You may also report the behavior to us at safety@theraisingclub.com.
termsPage.s19_p2: TRC reserves the right to cooperate with law enforcement in any investigation involving suspected harm to a child. We may suspend or permanently remove any account pending investigation of a safety report.
termsPage.s19_p3: TRC may also suspend or deactivate accounts for any conduct that, in our reasonable judgment, harms the safety, integrity, or reputation of our community, even if not explicitly listed as prohibited above.
termsPage.s20_p1: By you: You may close your account at any time by contacting us at hello@theraisingclub.com. Account closure terminates your access to the Platform and, subject to our data retention policy, leads to deletion of your personal data.
termsPage.s20_p2: By TRC: We may suspend or permanently deactivate your account, with or without notice, for violation of these Terms, non-payment, fraudulent activity, conduct harmful to the community, or any other reason at our sole discretion. Deactivated accounts may not re-register without our written permission.
termsPage.s20_p3: Sections that by their nature should survive termination (including Limitation of Liability, Indemnification, Dispute Resolution, and Intellectual Property) will survive.
termsPage.s21_p1: All content, design, software, trademarks, logos, and materials on the Platform (excluding User Content) are owned by or licensed to The Raising Club and are protected by intellectual property laws. You may not reproduce, distribute, modify, or create derivative works from any part of the Platform without our express written consent.
termsPage.s22_p1: Your use of the Platform is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which is incorporated into these Terms by reference. By using the Platform, you consent to the collection and use of your information as described in the Privacy Policy.
termsPage.s23_p1: THE PLATFORM IS PROVIDED AS IS AND AS AVAILABLE WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT. TRC DOES NOT WARRANT THAT THE PLATFORM WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED, ERROR-FREE, OR FREE OF HARMFUL COMPONENTS, OR THAT ANY CAREGIVER OR FAMILY IS SUITABLE, SAFE, OR FIT FOR YOUR NEEDS.
termsPage.s24_p1: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, THE RAISING CLUB AND ITS OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, EMPLOYEES, AND AFFILIATES SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO YOUR USE OF THE PLATFORM, INCLUDING PERSONAL INJURY, PROPERTY DAMAGE, OR HARM TO A CHILD ARISING FROM ANY CAREGIVER ENGAGEMENT ARRANGED THROUGH THE PLATFORM.
termsPage.s24_p2: OUR TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY TO YOU FOR ANY CLAIM ARISING FROM OR RELATED TO THESE TERMS OR THE PLATFORM SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF (A) THE TOTAL FEES YOU PAID TO TRC IN THE 12 MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM, OR (B) ONE HUNDRED US DOLLARS ($100).
termsPage.s24_p3: Some jurisdictions do not allow the exclusion of certain warranties or limitation of liability. In those jurisdictions, our liability is limited to the fullest extent permitted by law.
termsPage.s25_p1: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless The Raising Club and its affiliates, officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or related to: (a) your use of the Platform; (b) your User Content; (c) your violation of these Terms; (d) any childcare arrangement, employment, or interaction with another user that arises from your use of the Platform; or (e) your violation of any applicable law.
termsPage.s26_p1: Informal resolution first: Before filing any formal claim, you agree to contact us at hello@theraisingclub.com and give us 30 days to resolve the issue informally.
termsPage.s26_p2: Binding arbitration: If informal resolution fails, any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to these Terms or the Platform (except as set out below) shall be finally resolved by binding individual arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association (AAA) Commercial Arbitration Rules, with arbitration conducted in English. The arbitrators decision will be final and binding and may be entered as a judgment in any court of competent jurisdiction.
termsPage.s26_p3: Exceptions: Either party may seek emergency injunctive relief in a court of law to prevent irreparable harm, and claims related to intellectual property infringement are excluded from arbitration.
termsPage.s26_p4: Small claims: Either party may bring a claim in small claims court if the claim qualifies and remains in that court.
termsPage.s27_p1: YOU AND TRC AGREE THAT EACH MAY BRING CLAIMS AGAINST THE OTHER ONLY IN AN INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY, AND NOT AS A PLAINTIFF OR CLASS MEMBER IN ANY PURPORTED CLASS ACTION, CONSOLIDATED ACTION, OR REPRESENTATIVE PROCEEDING. If this waiver is found unenforceable, the arbitration agreement in Section 26 shall be void as to that claim.
termsPage.s28_p1: These Terms are governed by the laws of the United States and the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any court proceedings (where permitted under Section 26) shall be brought in the federal or state courts located in Delaware.
termsPage.s29_p1: We may update these Terms from time to time. When we make material changes, we will notify you by email or by a prominent notice on the Platform at least 15 days before the changes take effect. Your continued use of the Platform after the effective date constitutes your acceptance of the updated Terms. If you do not agree to the updated Terms, you must stop using the Platform and may close your account.
termsPage.s30_intro: Questions about these Terms? Reach us at:

privacyPage.header_intro: The Raising Club takes your privacy seriously and the privacy of the children in your care even more so. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and the choices you have.
privacyPage.s1_p1: The Raising Club operates the Platform at theraisingclub.com. When we say we, us, or our, we mean The Raising Club Marketplace. We are the data controller for personal information collected through the Platform.
privacyPage.s1_p2: Contact us with privacy questions at: privacy@theraisingclub.com
privacyPage.s2_1_li1: Name (first, last, and any preferred name)
privacyPage.s2_1_li2: Email address and password (hashed — we never store your password in plain text)
privacyPage.s2_1_li4: Location — neighborhood, city, ZIP code, or coordinates derived from your search input
privacyPage.s2_1_li7: Organization name and job title (for organization accounts)
privacyPage.s2_2_li1: Experience, skills, certifications, and care settings (self-reported)
privacyPage.s2_3_warning: We take childrens privacy very seriously. We collect only the minimum information about children needed to personalize the experience for parents. We do not allow children to create accounts.
privacyPage.s2_3_p1: Parents may enter the following information about each child in their care:
privacyPage.s2_3_li1: Birth month and birth year (we do not collect the full date of birth or the childs name)
privacyPage.s2_3_li2: A pet name or nickname chosen by the parent (not the childs real name)
privacyPage.s2_3_p2: This information is used only to calculate age ranges for matching purposes and to personalize the parents onboarding experience.
privacyPage.s2_3_p3: The Platform is not directed at children under 13. If we learn that we have inadvertently collected personal information from a child under 13 without verifiable parental consent, we will delete it promptly.
privacyPage.s2_3_p4: Additional Data Collection sections are fully described in your language.
privacyPage.s3_intro: We use the information we collect to:
privacyPage.s3_li1: Create and manage your account and provide the Platforms features
privacyPage.s3_li2: Match caregivers with families based on location, age groups, skills, and availability
privacyPage.s3_li3: Power the marketplace (job postings, applications, family listings, reviews)
privacyPage.s3_li5: Send transactional communications (account confirmations, booking confirmations, payment receipts)
privacyPage.s3_li6: Send community updates and newsletters (where you have opted in)
privacyPage.s3_li7: Respond to your support requests and investigate reported conduct
privacyPage.s3_li8: Detect and prevent fraud, abuse, and security threats
privacyPage.s3_li9: Analyze usage to improve Platform features and fix bugs
privacyPage.s3_note: We do not sell your personal information to third parties.
privacyPage.s4_intro: We share your information only as described below:
privacyPage.s4_1_p1: Your public profile (for caregivers and published family listings) is visible to other authenticated users.
privacyPage.s4_2_p1: We share data with trusted third-party providers who help us operate the Platform, including:
privacyPage.s4_2_li4: Resend / email provider — transactional email delivery
privacyPage.s4_2_li6: Background check providers — where background check services are used
privacyPage.s4_2_p2: These providers are contractually required to protect your data.
privacyPage.s4_3_p1: We may disclose your information when required by law, court order, or governmental authority.
privacyPage.s4_4_p1: If TRC is involved in a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, your information may be transferred as part of that transaction.
